what_it_does: A recollection/mapping note that catalogs the Company's UI 'archetypes' (reusable screen-renderer types), and for each one records its renderer build-status, its data source (the bridge API routes / corpus spaces it pulls from), and whether a LIVE region implementation already exists in surface/app to converge against rather than rebuild.
observations: Frames the central discriminator (attributed to 'DNA'): a surface screen is data-driven within an archetype, so the reuse unit is the ARCHETYPE (~5-7 renderers built once, instances cheap), not per-screen adapters.

Lists 8 screen-archetypes: single-unit immersive, piece-in-focus, canvas/board, ledger/list, dashboard cards+grid, compare, compose/build, home/landing — with codes like scr-piece, app-canvas, scr-compose.

For each archetype gives renderer-status (renderUnit and renderPiece marked BUILT/proven; the other 6 NOT built), the concrete data routes (e.g. /api/cognition/corpus, /api/graph, /api/cognition/list_runs, /api/build-intent), and a ★ marker where a live region already exists.
Separates three CROSS-CUTTING layers that are not screen-archetypes: controls (knobs/config), states (node-state-by-colour status layer), flows (the multi-screen walkthrough/sequence layer).
Includes a 'SCALE-TO-19' budget section: ~8 archetypes, 2 built, 6 to build, but 5 of the 6 already have live regions so the work is CONVERGE not greenfield.
Defines archetype per Tim's direction as factory(structure: slots/sockets) + DNA(render: visual) in one jointly-built codebase, so the 6 are built jointly with composition not by DNA alone.
Notes 'recollection's data under it' — corpus units, neighbours/pins, and recall to locate any screen's authoring intent.
standouts: The archetype-as-reuse-unit thesis: build ~5-7 renderers once, instances are data-driven and cheap.
The CONVERGE-not-greenfield insight: 5 of 6 unbuilt renderers already have live region implementations to reconcile against.
The archetype = factory(structure) + DNA(render) one-codebase formula attributed to Tim.
Explicit data-source-to-route grounding for every archetype, sourced from surface/app/src/api.ts.
conventions: Follows a recollection/data-layer-input note convention feeding 'the lead's interface catalog' and 'DNA's assembly'.
Uses ✅ for built renderers and ★ to mark existing live regions; references concrete /api/ routes and region/component names (NodeShape, registryStore, GreetingCard).
Frames itself as descriptive input (data-layer) for a downstream catalog, not the catalog itself.
concerns: Heavily shorthand and role-coded (DNA, the lead, recollection, scr-*/app-* codes) — opaque without the surrounding project vocabulary.
Renderer build-status and live-region existence are asserted but the note itself cannot verify current state of surface/app.
notes: This is the archetype→data-source→live-region crosswalk — read it to know which screen renderers exist, where each one's data comes from, and which are converge-vs-build.
questions: Who/what are 'DNA', 'the lead', and 'recollection' as roles in this build?
What is the SCALE-TO-19 target (19 screens?) and how do 8 archetypes scale to it?
Are the renderUnit/renderPiece 'BUILT/proven' claims still current?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it states it is the data-layer input mapping archetypes to data sources and live regions, and the body is exactly that crosswalk.
apparent_themes: Data-driven reuse via archetypes over per-screen code
Converge with existing live implementations rather than rebuild
Grounding UI plans in concrete API routes
intention_signals: It is trying to bound and de-risk a UI build by proving most screen renderers reduce to a small set of archetypes whose data already flows to live regions, so the build is a finite converge effort jointly with composition rather than open-ended greenfield work.
novelty: The archetype taxonomy paired with a per-archetype live-region/converge marker and explicit route grounding is a distinctive organizing artifact.
contribution: Contributes the screen-archetype taxonomy and the archetype→data-route→live-region crosswalk that a fused system would keep as the map of which renderers exist, what data each consumes, and which UI regions to converge versus build.
summary_for_embedding: A mapping note cataloging the Company's ~8 UI screen-archetypes with each one's renderer status, data-source API routes, and existing live-region implementation, plus three cross-cutting layers and a converge-not-greenfield build budget.
intention_for_embedding: To supply the data-layer input for the interface catalog by showing most screens reduce to a few reusable data-driven archetypes whose data already flows to live regions, making the UI build a finite converge effort.
